summary: The article is well-structured, informative, and tailored to the local context with accurate business data. Metadata is complete and relevant. There are minor issues with a misplaced lowercase letter and minor wording repetition, but no critical blockers. The CTAs are clear and natural, and there is good balance without AI-hype or filler. Overall the article is suitable for publishing with only minor polish recommended.

warnings.issue: Lowercase letter at the beginning of a sentence
warnings.evidence: In the Introduction: 'Two common tooth replacement options today are *dental implants* and *dentures*. Both serve to replace missing teeth but differ significantly in how they work, feel, and fit into your daily life. many patients ask about pain, cost, comfort, recovery time, oral health benefits, and any health restrictions.'
warnings.suggestion: Capitalize 'many' at the start of the sentence for proper grammar.

warnings.evidence: In the Introduction and Oral Health Implications sections, links use square brackets around only part of the phrase, e.g. [differences between dental](/dental-implants-vs-dentures-which-tooth-replacement-option-is-right-for-you).
warnings.suggestion: Consider linking the full phrase for clarity and SEO impact.

strengths: Metadata is present in the final article.
Accurate and complete metadata with relevant keywords targeting topic and location.
Correct use of the real local business name, phone number, email, and website, avoiding placeholders.
Clear, natural CTAs aligned with the business and location lead to effective conversion.
Balanced, evidence-based tone without AI-hype or filler language; professional and trustworthy.
Comprehensive coverage tailored to the dental implant vs denture topic without excess duplication of pillar content.
Good article structure with logical flow from introduction through benefits, considerations, cost, FAQs, and conclusion.

suggested_edits.issue: Lowercase first letter starting a sentence in Introduction.
suggested_edits.suggestion: Change 'many patients ask about pain...' to 'Many patients ask about pain...'.
Expand linked text so the whole phrase 'differences between dental implants and dentures' is linked for improved clarity.
